title: asplenium raoulii
intro: Asplenium raoulii, also known as Raouli, is a species of fern native to New Zealand. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it useful in treating wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Asplenium raoulii are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of Asplenium raoulii are typically made into a poultice or infused in hot water to be used as a tea or applied topically to affected areas.
